problem: Add a Finesse logo to the logon (sign-in) page. Currently, no Finesse branding assets exist anywhere in the repository, and the sign-in page renders only the Clerk <SignIn /> component inside a centered flex container with no custom branding.

files.why: The sign-in page component. Currently renders only Clerk's <SignIn /> with locale-aware path. This is the primary target for adding the Finesse logo.

The centered layout wrapping sign-in and sign-up pages. Uses 'flex min-h-screen items-center justify-center'. Logo could be added here to appear on both sign-in and sign-up, or only in the sign-in page.
Auth layout wrapping ClerkProvider with appearance/localization config. Boundary for auth-related styling.
The sign-up page component. Sibling to sign-in under the same centered layout. Relevant if the logo should appear on sign-up too.
Root locale layout. Defines favicon metadata and global CSS import. Boundary for app-wide branding.
Global CSS with Tailwind v4 layer ordering including 'clerk' layer. Relevant for any custom styling of the logo.
Demonstrates the established pattern for displaying logo images: next/image with static imports from @/public/assets/images/.
Directory where all image/logo assets are stored. A Finesse logo asset would need to be placed here.
English translations. Contains 'SignIn' namespace. Alt text or any user-visible strings for the logo should be added here.
French translations. Contains 'SignIn' namespace. Must be updated alongside en.json for i18n compliance.
App configuration including name ('Nextjs Starter'), locale settings, and Clerk localizations. Possible location for Finesse branding metadata.
Quality gate scripts: lint (ultracite), check:types (tsc), check:deps (knip), check:i18n (i18n-check), test (vitest), build.
Coding conventions: named exports only (except Next.js pages), Tailwind v4 utility classes, i18n rules (never hard-code user-visible strings), React conventions (no destructuring props).

facts: The sign-in page is at src/app/[locale]/(auth)/(center)/sign-in/[[...sign-in]]/page.tsx and renders only Clerk's <SignIn /> component with no custom branding (line 29).

The centered layout at src/app/[locale]/(auth)/(center)/layout.tsx wraps both sign-in and sign-up pages with a 'flex min-h-screen items-center justify-center' container and contains no logo or branding.
No file or string matching 'finesse' (case-insensitive) exists in the repository source code; only the ticket references it.
No Finesse logo asset (SVG, PNG, or any format) exists in public/assets/images/ or anywhere else in the repo.
Existing logo images are stored as static files in public/assets/images/ and imported using @/public/assets/images/ path aliases with next/image <Image> component (see Sponsors.tsx).
The project uses next-intl for i18n with 'en' and 'fr' locales; all user-visible strings must be in locale JSON files under the appropriate namespace (AGENTS.md convention).
Quality gates: lint via 'ultracite check --type-aware --type-check', typecheck via 'tsc --noEmit', dep check via 'knip', i18n check via 'i18n-check', unit tests via 'vitest run'.
Tailwind CSS v4 is used with CSS-first configuration (no tailwind.config.js); Clerk styles use a dedicated 'clerk' CSS layer.
The auth layout passes cssLayerName: 'clerk' to ClerkProvider appearance config for Tailwind v4 compatibility.
No existing tests cover the sign-in or sign-up pages in the tests/ directory.
unknowns: No Finesse logo asset is provided or attached to the ticket; it is unknown what format (SVG, PNG), dimensions, or design the logo should have.
It is unclear whether 'Finesse' refers to a product name, company name, or app rebrand; no context is given beyond the ticket title.
It is unknown whether the logo should appear only on the sign-in page or also on the sign-up page (both share the same centered layout).
The exact visual placement of the logo relative to the Clerk sign-in form is unspecified (above, below, beside, or overlaid).
It is unknown whether the existing app name 'Nextjs Starter' in AppConfig should be changed to 'Finesse'.

It is unclear whether the Finesse logo should replace the Clerk default branding within the <SignIn /> component (via Clerk appearance customization) or be added as a separate element outside the Clerk form.
